executive_summary: This report highlights opportunities to refine nonverbal communication. Focus on tightening gesture peaks, softening facial tone, and opening posture between points. Strengths include open-palm explanatory gestures and an alert room presence. Addressing these areas can enhance clarity and audience connection.

top_strengths.title: Open-palm explanatory delivery
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation. 00:15-00:30 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same open-palm gesture shape when emphasizing key ideas.

top_strengths.title: Alert room presence
top_strengths.evidence: 00:15-00:30 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room. 00:30-00:45 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same quick room checks that make the lecture feel attentive.

strength_inventory.title: Open-palm explanatory delivery
strength_inventory.evidence: 00:00-00:15 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation. 00:15-00:30 included open-palm explanatory gestures that supported the explanation.
strength_inventory.what_to_repeat: Keep the same open-palm gesture shape when emphasizing key ideas.

strength_inventory.title: Alert room presence
strength_inventory.evidence: 00:15-00:30 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room. 00:30-00:45 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room.

priority_actions.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Large bursts of motion can distract from the teaching point when they are not tightly timed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required. 00:15-00:30 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Keep big gestures for true emphasis and use smaller controlled hand movements elsewhere.

priority_actions.title: Soften the visible facial tone
priority_actions.why_it_matters: A more relaxed facial tone can make explanations feel warmer and less guarded.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 showed a flatter or tighter visible facial tone than the rest of the clip. 00:15-00:30 showed a flatter or tighter visible facial tone than the rest of the clip.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Reset the face between sentences and let the expression relax before the next point.

priority_actions.title: Open the stance between points
priority_actions.why_it_matters: A more open posture tends to read as more confident and easier to approach.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points. 00:30-00:45 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Let the elbows open slightly and release any folded or guarded arm positions between points.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Turn back toward the audience sooner
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:00-00:15 spent longer than needed turned away from the audience after checks. 00:15-00:30 spent longer than needed turned away from the audience after checks.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: After each board or note check, reset your shoulders and chin back toward the room.

keep_doing: Keep the same open-palm gesture shape when emphasizing key ideas.
Keep the same quick room checks that make the lecture feel attentive.
watch_for: Check whether gesture size matches the importance of the point instead of peaking on routine lines.
Check whether the face resets between points instead of staying tight through the whole sentence.
Check whether transitions keep the elbows and shoulders open instead of folding inward.
Check whether board or note checks end with a quick shoulder-and-chin reset back to the room.
confidence_notes: Face visibility is limited in parts of the clip, so eye-contact and facial-tone claims are less certain.
Face coverage dropped below 95%; audience orientation and facial scores are less stable.
Hand coverage dropped below 85%; gesture classification is less stable.

evidence_moments.headline: Low Audience Orientation
evidence_moments.observed_behavior: low audience orientation / closed posture / over animated delivery
evidence_moments.metric_evidence: Overall 43.1; room scan 64.3; presence 52.5; natural movement 15.8.
evidence_moments.qwen_interpretation: Qwen sees the teacher mainly addressing the audience; open-palm explanatory gestures recur; visible affect leans tense.
evidence_moments.coaching_implication: After each board or note check, reset your shoulders and chin back toward the room.
